Q3 2026 Outlook — Housing Risk Calendar
Three scenarios for Q3 2026:
🟢 Base case (~50%): CMBS defaults stay at 3-4/quarter. Multifamily refi cliff partially absorbed. GHRI 0.32-0.40.
🟡 Elevated (~35%): 5+ CMBS defaults in Q3 (KC_01 single trigger fires) OR multifamily delinquency >5.5%. GHRI 0.55-0.65. BX/CBRE severe bearish. SRS spikes.
🔴 Critical (<15%): Systemic CRE cascade + China property contagion to US bank balance sheets. GHRI 0.70+. Full domain bearish except SRS.
Primary watch: $85B multifamily refinancing cliff Q3-Q4 2026 + 5-default CMBS threshold.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1300  NORMAL

Global Housing Stress Canary operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. No major maritime chokepoint exposure identified in structural config.

Critical import dependencies include construction materials, lumber, steel, labor.

Primary exports — real estate assets, REIT instruments, mortgage securities — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1300 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.3500  WATCH

Global Housing Stress Canary is classified as a developed real estate market. Primary stress vector is global real estate bubble, US commercial real estate collapse, and China property developer crisis. Commercial real estate exposure is significant CRE concentration with elevated office and retail vacancy risk.

As a developed market, property values are sensitive to interest rate cycles — the aggressive central bank tightening of 2022-2024 has left mortgage holders with elevated refinancing risk. Commercial office markets face structural headwinds from remote work adoption globally.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for , pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  CMBS delinquency rate rise above 5% → commercial property stress signal  |  Mortgage refinancing cliff (2025-2027 vintage) → household stress spike  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3300  WATCH

Global Housing Stress Canary faces 4 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Wildfire, Flood, Earthquake. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon, Wildfire, Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in is deep and liquid, with well-capitalised domestic insurers and active reinsurance participation. Reinsurance profile: medium reinsurance dependency — tail events require offshore capacity.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3300 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
